name: aligned_zero_size
reason: aligned with size 0 should be rejected or undefined, not vacuously true via 0%0==0

name: empty_arch_satisfies_inv
reason: An architecture with zero layers is degenerate and should not satisfy the well-formedness invariant

name: upper_vaddr_not_above_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_not_above_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) <= base,
{
}
reason: upper_vaddr should always exceed base since num_entries>0 and entry_size>0 under inv; equality or less would mean zero or negative address span

name: entry_base_collapses_distinct_indices
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_base_collapses_distinct_indices(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < j,
        j <= arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: Different indices must yield different entry bases; collision would mean overlapping page table entries

name: entry_size_next_layer_inconsistent
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_size_next_layer_inconsistent(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) != arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat) * arch.num_entries((i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: Under inv the layer size decomposition must hold; if this verifies the invariant is internally contradictory
